# Infrawise

> MCP server for deterministic infrastructure analysis. Scans AWS services (DynamoDB, Lambda, SQS, SNS, Secrets Manager, SSM, CloudWatch, EventBridge, S3, API Gateway, RDS), databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB), and IaC (Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation) to surface findings and correct trigger event shapes ‚Äî without reading secret values or raw logs.

## Problem it solves

When an AI coding assistant writes a Lambda handler, it doesn't know:
- What tables, queues, or topics the function already accesses
- The correct SQS / SNS / EventBridge trigger event shape for the handler
- Whether a GSI or index exists for a query pattern
- Which secrets are missing rotation or which DLQs are unconfigured

Infrawise answers all of these via 16 MCP tools served over Streamable HTTP.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/Sidd27/infrawise/blob/main/README.md)
- [MCP tool reference](https://github.com/Sidd27/infrawise/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [AI agent instructions](https://github.com/Sidd27/infrawise/blob/main/AGENTS.md)

## Quick start

```bash
npm install -g infrawise
cd your-project
infrawise start --claude    # probe env + analyze + write .mcp.json + open Claude Code
# Every session after: just run "claude" ‚Äî editor manages the rest
```

For CI/CD (no editor) ‚Äî gate deploys on findings:
```bash
infrawise check --fail-on high    # exit 1 if any high-severity finding exists
```

For HTTP transport instead of stdio:
```bash
infrawise serve   # start MCP server at http://localhost:3000/mcp
```

## MCP tools (16 total)

- `get_infra_overview` ‚Äî full snapshot of all infrastructure, start here
- `get_graph_summary` ‚Äî complete graph: all nodes, edges, findings
- `analyze_function` ‚Äî per-function analysis with correct trigger event shapes
- `suggest_gsi` ‚Äî ready-to-use DynamoDB GSI definition
- `postgres_index_suggestions` ‚Äî exact CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY SQL
- `suggest_mongo_index` ‚Äî exact db.collection.createIndex command
- `mysql_index_suggestions` ‚Äî exact ALTER TABLE ADD INDEX SQL
- `get_queue_details` ‚Äî SQS queues with DLQ, encryption, FIFO type, and visibility timeout
- `get_topic_details` ‚Äî SNS topics with subscription count and filter policies (required message attributes)
- `get_secrets_overview` ‚Äî Secrets Manager rotation status (no values)
- `get_parameter_overview` ‚Äî SSM parameter names and types (no values)
- `get_lambda_overview` ‚Äî Lambda config, memory, timeout, trigger event shapes
- `get_eventbridge_details` ‚Äî EventBridge rules, schedules, target functions
- `get_s3_overview` ‚Äî S3 buckets, versioning, encryption, public access status
- `get_log_errors` ‚Äî CloudWatch error patterns (never raw log messages)
- `get_api_routes` ‚Äî API Gateway APIs (REST, HTTP, WebSocket) with routes, methods, and Lambda integrations

## Works with

Claude Code, Cursor, and any MCP-compatible client.

## What infrawise never does

- Never reads secret values or parameter values
- Never reads raw log messages
- Never writes to AWS or your database
- Never executes DDL
- No telemetry ‚Äî everything stays local
